description: Fix language switching: (1) AppShell.vue does not handle the 'change-lang' event from TheSidebar - wire it to call setLocale(). (2) SettingsView.vue panelLang does not sync with useI18n's currentLocale - changing one must update the other.
steps: In panel/web/admin/src/layouts/AppShell.vue: import setLocale from useI18n, add a handler function handleChangeLang(locale: string) that calls setLocale(locale as Locale), and bind @change-lang='handleChangeLang' on the TheSidebar component.

In panel/web/admin/src/views/SettingsView.vue: import { locale as currentLocale, setLocale } from useI18n. Add a watcher on panelLang that calls setLocale(panelLang.value) when panelLang changes. Also add a watcher on currentLocale that syncs panelLang.value = currentLocale.value (so sidebar changes reflect in settings). Initialize panelLang from currentLocale.value on mount instead of only from API response.
Ensure the Locale type import is available in AppShell.vue (import type { Locale } from '@koris/composables/useI18n').
acceptance_criteria: Changing language in sidebar immediately updates the UI locale and the settings page language select
Changing language in settings page immediately updates the UI locale and the sidebar select reflects it
Both use the shared setLocale() function so localStorage is persisted and RTL direction updates
findings: Type inference required explicit ref<string> for panelLang since currentLocale.value is typed as Locale. The loadPanelSettings API returns a plain string that gets assigned to panelLang, so explicit string typing prevents TS2322. Bidirectional watchers use guard checks to prevent infinite loops.
